<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

```yaml
# =============================================================================
# AGENT WORKING MEMORY SCHEMA
# Food Delivery Platform — Polyglot Microservices
# Primary: Java/Spring Boot | Secondary: Kotlin, Python, Go, TypeScript
# =============================================================================
# HOW TO USE THIS FILE:
#   - The agent populates and updates this file across turns in a coding session.
#   - Each section has a schema definition (commented) followed by a live `state:`
#     block that the agent writes to.
#   - Fields marked REQUIRED must always be populated when the section is active.
#   - Fields marked OPTIONAL may be omitted when not applicable.
#   - Never edit the schema comment blocks; only update the `state:` blocks.
# =============================================================================


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 1 — SESSION STATE
# Tracks the top-level task the agent is currently working on, what phase of
# work is active, and a breadcrumb trail of completed steps for recovery after
# context interruption.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
session_state:

  # SCHEMA:
  #   task_id:        REQUIRED | string  | Short unique slug for the current task, e.g. "add-refund-endpoint"
  #   task_summary:   REQUIRED | string  | One-sentence human-readable description of the goal
  #   status:         REQUIRED | enum    | idle | in_progress | blocked | awaiting_review | complete
  #   phase:          REQUIRED | enum    | exploration | design | implementation | testing | review | done
  #   active_service: OPTIONAL | enum    | api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service | cross-service
  #   current_file:   OPTIONAL | string  | Absolute-style repo path of the file being actively edited
  #   last_action:    REQUIRED | string  | One sentence: what the agent did most recently
  #   next_action:    REQUIRED | string  | One sentence: what the agent will do next
  #   breadcrumbs:    OPTIONAL | list    | Ordered list of completed steps; used to resume after interruption
  #     - step:       REQUIRED | int     | Step number (monotonically increasing)
  #       summary:    REQUIRED | string  | What was accomplished in this step
  #       outcome:    REQUIRED | enum    | success | partial | failed | skipped

  state:
    task_id: ""
    task_summary: ""
    status: idle
    phase: exploration
    active_service: null
    current_file: null
    last_action: "Session initialised; no task started yet."
    next_action: "Await task assignment from the user."
    breadcrumbs: []


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 2 — DECISIONS MADE
# Immutable log of architectural and technical decisions taken during the
# session. Each entry captures the WHAT, WHY, alternatives rejected, and any
# constraints from this codebase that drove the choice.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
decisions_made:

  # SCHEMA (each entry in the list):
  #   id:              REQUIRED | string  | Slug, e.g. "use-optimistic-locking"
  #   timestamp:       REQUIRED | string  | ISO-8601 UTC, e.g. "2025-01-15T10:32:00Z"
  #   service:         REQUIRED | enum    | api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service | cross-service
  #   layer:           REQUIRED | enum    | model | service | controller | repository | gateway | messaging | infra | test
  #   decision:        REQUIRED | string  | What was decided (the WHAT)
  #   rationale:       REQUIRED | string  | Why this was chosen (the WHY); reference domain concepts where applicable
  #   alternatives:    OPTIONAL | list<string> | Other options that were considered and why they were rejected
  #   constraints_applied: OPTIONAL | list<string> | Pitfalls, forbidden operations, or domain rules that constrained the choice
  #     # Examples from this codebase:
  #     # - "OrderStatus.isValidTransition() must not be weakened"
  #     # - "Stripe uses MANUAL capture — payment must be explicitly captured post-ACCEPTED"
  #     # - "Inventory Redis lock must be acquired before any stock mutation"
  #     # - "JWT secret config must not be changed"
  #   affects_files:   OPTIONAL | list<string> | Repo paths of files impacted by this decision
  #   reversible:      REQUIRED | bool    | Whether this decision can be easily undone

  state:
    decisions: []
    # Example entry (uncomment and populate when recording a real decision):
    # - id: "example-decision"
    #   timestamp: "2025-01-15T10:32:00Z"
    #   service: order-service
    #   layer: service
    #   decision: "Use Stripe PaymentIntent capture in OrderService.acceptOrder() rather than auto-capture."
    #   rationale: >
    #     The Stripe integration is configured with MANUAL capture (capture_method=manual).
    #     The stripePaymentIntentId is stored on the Order entity. Capturing at ACCEPTED
    #     status aligns with the OrderStatus state machine: PENDING → ACCEPTED is the
    #     first valid transition where payment confirmation is appropriate.
    #   alternatives:
    #     - "Auto-capture at PaymentIntent creation: rejected because the order may be cancelled before acceptance, requiring an immediate refund."
    #   constraints_applied:
    #     - "OrderStatus.isValidTransition() enforces PENDING → ACCEPTED; capture must only happen on this transition."
    #     - "stripePaymentIntentId (pi_xxxx) must be present on the Order before capture is attempted."
    #     - "STRIPE_API_KEY must never be hardcoded; read from environment variable only."
    #   affects_files:
    #     - "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/OrderService.java"
    #     - "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/model/OrderStatus.java"
    #   reversible: false


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 3 — FILES MODIFIED
# Tracks every file touched in this session, why it was changed, what kind of
# change was made, and whether the change has been verified by a test run.
# Restricted-write paths are called out explicitly to prevent accidents.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
files_modified:

  # SCHEMA (each entry in the list):
  #   path:            REQUIRED | string  | Repo-relative path, e.g. "order-service/src/main/java/.../OrderService.java"
  #   service:         REQUIRED | enum    | api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service | root
  #   language:        REQUIRED | enum    | java | kotlin | python | go | typescript | yaml | properties | other
  #   change_type:     REQUIRED | enum    | created | modified | deleted | renamed
  #   change_summary:  REQUIRED | string  | One sentence describing what changed
  #   reason:          REQUIRED | string  | Why this file was changed; link to a decisions_made.id where applicable
  #   is_restricted:   REQUIRED | bool    | True if path appears in restricted_write_paths — flag for human review
  #   test_coverage:   REQUIRED | enum    | covered | partial | none | not_applicable
  #     # covered        = at least one test directly exercises the changed code path
  #     # partial        = some changed lines are tested but not all branches
  #     # none           = no test currently covers this change
  #     # not_applicable = config, lock file, or generated file
  #   related_tests:   OPTIONAL | list<string> | Paths of test files that cover this change
  #   build_system:    OPTIONAL | enum    | maven | gradle | pip | go-modules | npm
  #     # Reminder: order-service=Maven, api-gateway=Gradle, inventory-service=pip,
  #     #           driver-service=go-modules, notification-service=npm

  state:
    files: []
    # Example entry:
    # - path: "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/OrderService.java"
    #   service: order-service
    #   language: java
    #   change_type: modified
    #   change_summary: "Added refund logic in cancelOrder() that calls Stripe refund API using stripePaymentIntentId."
    #   reason: "Implements decision 'add-refund-endpoint'; refunds must reference the stored pi_xxxx ID per Stripe MANUAL capture flow."
    #   is_restricted: false
    #   test_coverage: partial
    #   related_tests:
    #     - "order-service/src/test/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/OrderServiceTest.java"
    #   build_system: maven


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 4 — TESTS RUN
# Records every test execution during the session: command used, which service,
# pass/fail counts, and any failures that need follow-up. Tracks coverage
# deltas where available.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
tests_run:

  # SCHEMA (each entry in the list):
  #   run_id:          REQUIRED | int     | Monotonically increasing run number within the session
  #   timestamp:       REQUIRED | string  | ISO-8601 UTC
  #   service:         REQUIRED | enum    | api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service
  #   framework:       REQUIRED | enum    | junit | pytest | go-test | jest
  #     # junit  → order-service   (cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress)
  #     # pytest → inventory-service
  #     # go-test → driver-service
  #     # jest   → notification-service
  #   command:         REQUIRED | string  | Exact shell command executed
  #   trigger:         REQUIRED | enum    | pre-change-baseline | post-change-verification | regression-check | ci-simulation
  #   result:          REQUIRED | enum    | pass | fail | error | skipped
  #   total_tests:     OPTIONAL | int     | Total number of test cases discovered
  #   passed:          OPTIONAL | int
  #   failed:          OPTIONAL | int
  #   errors:          OPTIONAL | int     | Tests that errored (distinct from assertion failures)
  #   skipped:         OPTIONAL | int
  #   duration_seconds: OPTIONAL | float
  #   failures:        OPTIONAL | list    | Details of individual failures
  #     - test_name:   REQUIRED | string  | Fully qualified test name
  #       file:        REQUIRED | string  | Source file path
  #       message:     REQUIRED | string  | Failure message or exception summary
  #       is_new:      REQUIRED | bool    | True if this failure did not exist before the current session's changes
  #   coverage:        OPTIONAL | object
  #     overall_pct:   OPTIONAL | float   | Overall line/branch coverage percentage
  #     delta_pct:     OPTIONAL | float   | Change vs. pre-session baseline (positive = improved)
  #     uncovered_paths: OPTIONAL | list<string> | Key uncovered code paths worth noting
  #   notes:           OPTIONAL | string  | Any contextual notes (e.g., "Redis not available; lock tests skipped")

  state:
    runs: []
    # Example entry:
    # - run_id: 1
    #   timestamp: "2025-01-15T11:00:00Z"
    #   service: order-service
    #   framework: junit
    #   command: "cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress"
    #   trigger: post-change-verification
    #   result: fail
    #   total_tests: 42
    #   passed: 41
    #   failed: 1
    #   errors: 0
    #   skipped: 0
    #   duration_seconds:
